focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Charlene mispronounces 'Belle Epoque' and questions its exclusivity to Lexington; Micky confirms it's playing there and heard it's good from a coworker, as they enter the theater.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charlene tends to Micky's injuries and arranges a date with him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene re-bandages Micky's facial wounds, asks about his fight loss, and they agree to go out for dinner and a movie.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlene and Micky are in the car driving to their dinner and movie date.
evidence_quotes: Charlene carefully re-bandages the left side of his eye and cheek bone.
So are you gonna take me to dinnah and movie tonight or what?
Why'd you stand me up last night?
Did the Jewish black guy do this to you?

relation_updates: Charlene and Micky solidify their connection through care and shared conversation, agreeing to a date.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Charlene's presence sparks tension as Alice and Dicky feel disrespected; Micky defends her, seeking paid training, while Dicky offers support to keep the family united.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Advocate for Micky's paid training in Vegas
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene accuses Alice and Dicky of failing to support Micky's training and proposes Vegas for paid year-round training, leading Dicky to offer family-funded training to keep Micky.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have Micky receive paid training in Vegas
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlene is at the center of family tension after Dicky offers an alternative to Vegas, with awkwardness among the group.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Micky will choose Vegas training or accept Dicky's offer of family-funded training
evidence_quotes: CHARLENE: I don't know, maybe you not showin' up on time to train. Maybe Micky havin' to come find you in a crack HOUSE--

relation_updates: Relationship with Alice and Dicky becomes more hostile
Micky defends her against family

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky, injured and drinking, seeks Charlene's support but is rejected; she criticizes his surrender to family pressures. Dicky endures brutal crack withdrawal in prison. Months later, Micky remains depressed, roofing with friends.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charlene rejecting Micky's visit and criticizing his family's influence
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene refuses to see Micky via intercom, states she won't be dragged down by his family, and criticizes his decision to quit boxing as stupid.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To avoid interaction with Micky and voice her disapproval
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky walks away from the intercom after being rejected
evidence_quotes: I don't wanna see you, Micky.
I can't take it, I'm not gonna let you or your family drag me down.
That's fuckin' stupid.
Because it's sad you let them take it away from you, with all their stupid bullshit.

relation_updates: Rejects Micky
Criticizes Micky's passivity and family influence

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alice watches a documentary with family, upset over Dicky's situation; Micky, emotional and conflicted, reconnects with Charlene, spends the night together, then leaves early with boxing gear.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Reconciled girlfriend
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene arrives at Micky's apartment, they reconcile emotionally and physically, spend the night together, and Micky leaves early with boxing gear.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlene wakes up alone in Micky's t-shirt and goes out after he leaves with his gym bag.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Micky will return to boxing and if their reconciliation will last.
evidence_quotes: KNOCKING ON THE DOOR, HE OPENS IT AS HE LISTENS TO PHONE—CHARLENE IS THERE.
He and Charlene stare at each other, taking it in, emotional, awkward.
She laughs a little through tears. They hug.
Charlene sits up in bed, groggy, gets up in Micky's t-shirt, goes out.

relation_updates: Reconciles with Micky
Spends the night with Micky

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charlene, Micky's girlfriend, who verbally argues with his family and physically defends herself during an attack.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene asserts Micky's autonomy against Alice, is insulted as a 'skank' by the sisters, and fights back physically when Alice lunges at her, leading Micky to intervene and break up the altercation.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The physical fight is stopped by Micky, and Charlene remains with him, but the family conflict is unresolved and tense.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Hostility with Micky's family persists after the physical confrontation.
evidence_quotes: Micky's a grown man, he can make his own decisions.
Don't ever lay a hand on Charlene.
Don't call me skank, I'll rip that hair right outta ya fuckin' head!
Charlene grabs Red Dog's face and shoves her... then backhands Beaver, who's grabbing her hair, sloppy like a bar fight.

relation_updates: Micky explicitly defends Charlene against his mother
Charlene's relationship with Micky's family becomes violently hostile
status_updates: Charlene becomes a target of physical aggression from Micky's family
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Assertive in defending Micky's choices and herself against insults and aggression.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky's girlfriend celebrating his knockout victory and the announcement of his world title shot
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene reacts with intense celebration to Micky's knockout win over Sanchez and later learns he has earned a world title shot
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Charlene is happy and supportive of Micky's victory and title opportunity, as shown by her actions
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Charlene is in the dressing room celebrating with others after Micky's win and the title shot news
evidence_quotes: CHARLENE (jumping up & down) YEAHHHHHH! FUCK YEAHHHHHHH!
Charlene jumps up and down crying and laughing.
Charlene kisses Micky.

relation_updates: Charlene and Micky celebrate his victory with a kiss
Charlene witnesses Sal LoNano confirming Micky's title shot
status_updates: Charlene's partner now has a world title opportunity

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dicky leaves the gym after a confrontation, ignoring George's pleas to return; he walks through Lowell as people recognize him, while George worries about his intentions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Charlene, Micky's girlfriend, opposes Dicky's return to the training team.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene walks out of the gym after Micky chooses to reinstate Dicky, ending her direct involvement in his training.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To keep Dicky away from Micky's training.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Outside the gym, separated from Micky's training circle.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether she will return to support Micky or maintain her opposition.
evidence_quotes: CHARLENE They have to go, Micky.
CHARLENE We saved him! Dickya junkbag!
She looks at him, turns and walks out of the gym, followed by O'Keefe, who looks sadly back at Micky.

relation_updates: Relationship with Micky strained by his choice to prioritize Dicky
Hostility toward Dicky intensifies as she calls him a 'junkbag'
status_updates: Removed herself from Micky's training team
Lost influence over Micky's boxing decisions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky wins, celebrated by Dicky, Charlene, and Alice; brothers honored as champions. Micky retires in 2003 after fights with Gatti; Dicky stays sober, trains fighters. Micky marries Charlene, lives in Lowell with family.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky Ward's romantic partner
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Charlene kisses Micky deeply after his championship win, and their marriage in 2005 is confirmed via text overlay.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In a romantic relationship with Micky Ward, with marriage announced for 2005.
evidence_quotes: Charlene... Alice... George all screaming with joy outside the ring...
when Micky is pulled toward Charlene, who grabs his face and kisses him deeply,
'Micky married Charlene in 2005.

relation_updates: Romantic bond with Micky Ward affirmed
Future marriage to Micky Ward established
status_updates: Present at Micky's championship victory

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Charlene begins as Micky Ward's new girlfriend, confronting him after a missed date and quickly building a bond through care and shared plans. She becomes a fierce advocate for his career independence, leading to conflicts with his family and periods of rejection and reconciliation. Ultimately, she supports his championship victory, and they marry, solidifying her role in his life.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 2、EXT. MICKY'S APARTMENT ABOVE GARAGE - MORNING - LATER IN THIB
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky considers Mike Toma's offer to train in Vegas but hesitates due to loyalty to his brother Dicky and family, who express distrust and concern over leaving them behind.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Dicky, addicted to crack, hiding at a crack house to avoid family detection.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky is warned his mother is outside, jumps from a second-floor window to escape, injures his back, and is confronted by Micky.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Avoid being caught by his mother while high on crack.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Back injured from the jump, still attempting to hide his addiction from family.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Family's discovery of Dicky's crack addiction remains averted but imminent.
evidence_quotes: WOLFIE (shouts from downstairs) Dicky, YOUR MUTHUAH'S OUTSIDE!
Dicky climbs out the window and jumps...
lands hard on top of some garbage bags...Bounces onto the filthy ground. He rolls over, his back fucked.
DICKY (WHISPERS) Don't tell Ma I'm here, pick me up back at the house, I'll go through the yahds -

relation_updates: caught deceiving family about drug use
confronted by Micky about being at crack house
status_updates: back injured from window jump
high on crack for days
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Shows fear and evasion when mother arrives, denies presence at crack house.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 2、EXT. MICKY'S APARTMENT ABOVE GARAGE - MORNING - LATER IN THIB
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky considers Mike Toma's offer to train in Vegas but hesitates due to loyalty to his brother Dicky and family, who express distrust and concern over leaving them behind.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: cornerman providing support and coaching during Micky's boxing match
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky jumps into the ring to tend to Micky between rounds, coaches him to jab and move, and accuses him of fighting scared
timeline_nodes.goal_state: motivate Micky to fight effectively and stay competitive
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky remains bloodied, gets knocked down, and continues to be beaten despite Dicky's instructions
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Micky unable to execute the jab-and-move strategy against the larger opponent
evidence_quotes: Dicky jumps into the ring
Micky gets back onto his feet, looks into his corner at Dicky screaming, "Hold on! Hold on!"

relation_updates: provides corner support during crisis
accuses Micky of fighting scared
status_updates: in the ring attending to Micky between rounds
auxiliary.persona_anchor: actively involved in cornering but his coaching does not change the fight's outcome

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 2、EXT. MICKY'S APARTMENT ABOVE GARAGE - MORNING - LATER IN THIB
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky considers Mike Toma's offer to train in Vegas but hesitates due to loyalty to his brother Dicky and family, who express distrust and concern over leaving them behind.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky's brother and trainer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After Micky's fight loss, Dicky approaches Sugar Ray Leonard to pitch his own comeback and HBO documentary, shifting focus from Micky's situation.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get Sugar Ray Leonard involved in his comeback fight and HBO film.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dicky is promoting his return but has not secured Sugar Ray's participation; he remains with Micky's team as HBO films.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Sugar Ray Leonard will agree to Dicky's request.
evidence_quotes: DICKY (looks at Sugar Ray like he's fucking crazy; what ELSE) My comeback.
Dicky walks toward Sugar Ray Leonard
leaving Dicky & Alice standing alone under the bright neon lights. HBO camera still filming...

relation_updates: re-established contact with Sugar Ray Leonard
asserted his own boxing narrative to HBO crew
status_updates: being filmed by HBO for documentary about his comeback
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Dicky prioritizes self-promotion over his brother's loss.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 2、EXT. MICKY'S APARTMENT ABOVE GARAGE - MORNING - LATER IN THIB
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky considers Mike Toma's offer to train in Vegas but hesitates due to loyalty to his brother Dicky and family, who express distrust and concern over leaving them behind.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Dicky acts as a protective brother who feels threatened by Mike Toma's offer to Micky.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky sees Micky talking to Mike Toma, attempts to intervene but is held back by Alice, then warns Micky about Toma and asks if he was included in the offer.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prevent Micky from accepting Toma's outside training offer and keep him within the family's influence.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky indicates he rejected Toma's offer due to family loyalty, maintaining Dicky's involvement in his career.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The threat of outside promoters like Mike Toma trying to recruit Micky away from his family.
evidence_quotes: DICKY TURNS AND SEES MICKY TALKING TO AN OLDER GUY, MIKE TOMA, FEET AWAY.
DICKY You can't trust that guy, Micky, you don't know 'im. Did he say anythin' about me? Did he say I could come?
MICKY (looking out window) No, he didn't mention you.

relation_updates: expressed distrust toward Mike Toma
questioned whether he was included in Toma's offer
status_updates: felt excluded from potential career-changing opportunity for Micky
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Dicky shows distrust towards outsiders and concern over his own exclusion from Micky's opportunities.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dicky, after robbing a businessman, flees on foot when a cop car arrives and a bald businessman identifies him as the perpetrator.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: addict robbing a businessman to fund drug purchase
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Robbery interrupted when a previous victim identifies him, leading to police arrival and flight
timeline_nodes.goal_state: obtain cash to buy crack cocaine
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: fleeing on foot after being identified during the robbery
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he will escape the police
evidence_quotes: DICKY (through cheap megaphone SLURRING) Get outta the cah put ya hands on the roof!
[Takes cash, mumbles to Karen] Take half, get us some rocks.
BALD BUSINESSMAN THAT'S THE GUY, HE GOT ME AND HE'S DOIN' IT AGAIN!
Dicky takes off running away...

adversarial relationship with bald businessman who identifies him
status_updates: now a wanted criminal identified as repeat offender
fleeing from police on foot

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky, injured and drinking, seeks Charlene's support but is rejected; she criticizes his surrender to family pressures. Dicky endures brutal crack withdrawal in prison. Months later, Micky remains depressed, roofing with friends.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: addict assaulting bouncer and police during arrest

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: arrested and held on the ground by police

COP CHASING HIM on foot runs up, Dicky PUNCHES HIM OUT. Busting open his nose, blood pouring.
Dicky takes a swing at ANOTHER COP.
Officers jump out and slam Dicky to the ground! Dicky screams, fighting back, kicking and punching the police arresting him...

in direct conflict with police officers

facing imprisonment and forced withdrawal

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky, injured and drinking, seeks Charlene's support but is rejected; she criticizes his surrender to family pressures. Dicky endures brutal crack withdrawal in prison. Months later, Micky remains depressed, roofing with friends.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: inmate going through crack withdrawal in prison
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Endures crack withdrawal in prison cell, screaming and shaking until he becomes clean
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: clean from crack after enduring withdrawal
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his prison sentence, his HBO movie, and his boxing comeback
evidence_quotes: Dicky shivers through crack withdrawal, clinging to his bed, sweating.
SCREAMS, scratches at himself, SCREAMS in agony.
Time lapse shows day night day night as Dicky shakes, screams, shivers. Til he stops - exhausted. Clean.

status_updates: Dicky goes through crack withdrawal and becomes clean
auxiliary.persona_anchor: reaches sobriety through forced withdrawal in prison

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 13、INT. ALICE'S HOUSE - 3 WEEKS LATER - HBO PROMO FOR 'HIGH EB
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dicky moves unseen through the dim cafeteria, exits through unlocked metal doors, descending stairs to an unknown area below, continuing his solitary, ghost-like journey within the facility.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: inmate reacting to past footage and attempting phone contact, then moving through facility
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Watches HBO footage of himself and family, attempts to call mother but gets busy signal, then moves unseen through cafeteria to open an unlocked door to stairs below.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to contact his mother by phone
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: opens unlocked metal mesh door to stairs leading to an unknown area below cafeteria
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dicky's intention in moving to the unknown area is not stated
evidence_quotes: Dicky stands there for a moment watching himself, his son, his life on TV... starts shaking his head no no no no.
He hears a busy signal, operator say, "Sorry, that line is busy, sir."
Nobody even sees Dicky, a ghost floating through the gloom.

relation_updates: failed attempt to reach mother Alice by phone
status_updates: moves through facility unnoticed like a ghost
auxiliary.persona_anchor: shows emotional distress and moves unnoticed like a ghost

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Released from prison and reunites with family
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky is released from prison, reunites with his mother and son, and expresses intent to apologize to Micky, but Alice insists he has nothing to apologize for.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Free and with family, with his ego reinforced by Alice's dismissal of the need to apologize.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Dicky will apologize to Micky and how he will reintegrate.
evidence_quotes: DICKY (SCREAMING OUT) YEAHHHHHH! MY BRUTHAH DROPPED SANCHEZ!
Dicky walks out of prison into the bright sun
ALICE (looks at Dicky like he's CRAZY) What are you talkin' about? You're Dicky Ecklund. You got nuthin' to apologize for.

relation_updates: Dicky reunites with his mother and son upon release
Alice tells Dicky he has nothing to apologize for, reinforcing his ego
status_updates: Dicky is released from prison
Dicky's status as a boxing advisor is validated by Micky's victory
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Displays unchanged ego, taking credit for Micky's victory and accepting Alice's view that he owes no apology.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: returning brother and trainer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky returns to the gym, apologizes to everyone, and declares his readiness to train Micky again, but Micky informs him that he has promised not to work with Dicky.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to resume training Micky
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dicky is back in the gym and sober, welcomed by the community, but excluded from training Micky due to Micky's promise.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Dicky's wish to train Micky conflicts with Micky's promise to others not to work with Dicky.
evidence_quotes: DICKY I wanna make an amend to everyone here, I know I done a lot of stupid things, and I wanna say I'm sorry, and that's ovah.
MICKY I made a promise to them that I wouldn't work with you again.

gym community welcomes Dicky back
status_updates: Dicky is back in the gym and sober
Dicky is excluded from training Micky

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky wins, celebrated by Dicky, Charlene, and Alice; brothers honored as champions. Micky retires in 2003 after fights with Gatti; Dicky stays sober, trains fighters. Micky marries Charlene, lives in Lowell with family.

timeline_nodes.goal_state: to support Micky's boxing career by repairing his relationship with Charlene and staying sober
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dicky has secured Charlene's cooperation for Micky, committed to sobriety, and been assigned to retrieve O'Keefe

OK. I'll see ya in Micky's corner, otherwise go fuck yourself.
Good. Get O'Keefe back, too.

Micky accepts Dicky's intervention and reaffirms Dicky's role in his career
status_updates: Dicky commits to quitting drugs

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky wins, celebrated by Dicky, Charlene, and Alice; brothers honored as champions. Micky retires in 2003 after fights with Gatti; Dicky stays sober, trains fighters. Micky marries Charlene, lives in Lowell with family.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: brother, cornerman, and trainer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Dicky celebrates Micky's championship win and is shown in a freeze frame living soberly with family, training fighters, and battling addiction daily.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sober, living with Alice and Little Dicky in Lowell, training fighters, and battling addiction every day.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing daily battle with addiction.
evidence_quotes: .Dicky shaking his fist, screaming Micky on, "This is it! This is it! This is it!"

status_updates: free from crack, trains fighters in Lowell

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Dicky Ward, Micky's older brother and trainer, is a crack addict whose dysfunction leads to family conflict, crime, and violent arrest. After getting clean in prison, he is released but initially rejected by Micky before brokering a truce and committing to sobriety. He supports Micky's championship win and lives soberly, though still battling addiction daily.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

Micky stumbles, hurt, almost goes down, blood spilling down his nose, mouth, face
All he can do is cover up, holding his gloves close to his face...
Micky goes sprawling across the ring, stumbling backwards...Falling down hard onto the canvas!

Micky sustains facial injuries

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky leaves his apartment with his gym bag, heading out early in the morning.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Tension with mother Alice over Dicky's situation and his renewed focus on boxing.
evidence_quotes: KNOCKING ON THE DOOR, HE OPENS IT AS HE LISTENS TO PHONE—CHARLENE IS THERE.
He and Charlene stare at each other, taking it in, emotional, awkward. She laughs a little through tears. They hug.
Micky stands at the top of the stairs, old blue gym bag slung over his shoulder, early morning.

relation_updates: Reconciles with Charlene
Tension with mother Alice over Dicky's situation
status_updates: Emotionally vulnerable
Takes action to resume boxing
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Emotionally vulnerable boxer taking action to resume training.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky Ward restarts boxing training under new management with a condition to exclude his brother and mother, leading to a family confrontation.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Micky trains at the gym, is introduced to manager Sal LoNano by trainer Mickey O'Keefe, who sets a condition of excluding Dicky and Alice. Later, Micky is confronted by his mother and sisters at Charlene's apartment.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To regain boxing confidence with a local fight under Sal LoNano's management.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky has secured new management and a training plan, but his family is angry and confronts him at Charlene's apartment.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Micky can enforce O'Keefe's condition to exclude Dicky and Alice, and how to handle the ongoing family conflict.
evidence_quotes: Nobody keeps tryin' like this kid, I swear to God someone up there's watchin' over him, he's far from done.
No Dicky, no Alice. If they come back, I'm gone, Mick.
I already made some calls. Spoke to Bobby Tags and he says we can get a small fight, somethin' local to staht back with.
You can't hide from us, Micky.

relation_updates: Micky forms a professional relationship with Sal LoNano
Micky's relationship with Mickey O'Keefe becomes conditional on excluding Dicky and Alice
Micky's relationship with his mother and sisters becomes more strained due to the confrontation
status_updates: Micky is now managed by Sal LoNano and trained by Mickey O'Keefe
Micky is preparing for a local fight to regain confidence
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A boxer asserting professional independence while facing family opposition.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky expresses conflicted emotions, showing relief at Dicky's recovery but lingering pain and regret, ending with a silent nod before leaving.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: victor of the fight
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Micky knocks out Sanchez and is told by Sal LoNano that he has earned a world title shot against Shea Neary.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to win the world title
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky is in the dressing room celebrating his victory and the news of his title shot.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The specific terms and details of the title shot are not yet discussed.
evidence_quotes: Micky runs to a neutral corner; then jumps with his arms in the air when Sanchez does not move.
SAL LONANO Micky, Mike Toma just told me something very interesting.
SAL LONANO Now it's Micky's shot. Shea Neary in London for the title.
Micky smiling in the middle of the celebration, then, distracted, something's missing.

relation_updates: Micky pushes Sal away during celebration, indicating possible tension
status_updates: wins the fight by knockout
becomes a world title contender
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Boxer who has won a fight and secured a championship opportunity.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dicky leaves the gym after a confrontation, ignoring George's pleas to return; he walks through Lowell as people recognize him, while George worries about his intentions.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky reinstates Dicky as trainer after acknowledging his contribution to past victories.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Micky states he wouldn't have won without Dicky and decides to bring him back, leading Charlene and O'Keefe to leave the gym.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To train with a unified team for his next fight.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dicky is back in the gym sparring with Micky, while Charlene and O'Keefe have departed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Charlene and O'Keefe will return to his training team.
evidence_quotes: MICKY I wouldn'ta won Sanchez without Dicky.
MICKY I want Dicky back. And I want you, Charlene, and I want O'Keefe, and my family.
The two brothers spar, falling right back into it as Alice, George, others watch.

relation_updates: Reinstates Dicky as trainer
Conflicts with Charlene and O'Keefe who leave
status_updates: Asserts authority as primary decision-maker for his career
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Fighter asserting control over his training decisions.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky wins, celebrated by Dicky, Charlene, and Alice; brothers honored as champions. Micky retires in 2003 after fights with Gatti; Dicky stays sober, trains fighters. Micky marries Charlene, lives in Lowell with family.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Micky privately reconciles with Dicky and publicly controls the press conference as a professional fighter.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Micky tells Dicky he was his hero after Dicky's question, and at the weigh-in, Micky insists only he and his manager go on stage, then includes his parents publicly.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Preparing for the WBU title fight against Shea Neary.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Micky is at the weigh-in with family support, positioned as a confident contender ready for the fight.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The upcoming championship fight against Shea Neary.
evidence_quotes: MICKY You went ten rounds, and he never hurt you. You were my hero.
MICKY HEY. Nobody goes on stage. Just me and the manager, that's how it's done by the professionals.
MICKY (SHOUTS OUT) Hey, Ma, Dad, come on up here!
MICKY (to the reporters) My muthah used to manage both of us, me and Dicky both.

relation_updates: Emotionally reconciles with Dicky by calling him his hero
Publicly honors his mother's role in his career
status_updates: Operates as professional fighter managing media
Includes family on his own terms at public event
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Professional fighter who manages his public image and includes family on his own terms.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Micky wins, celebrated by Dicky, Charlene, and Alice; brothers honored as champions. Micky retires in 2003 after fights with Gatti; Dicky stays sober, trains fighters. Micky marries Charlene, lives in Lowell with family.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Boxer who wins the world title against Shea Neary and is celebrated in the ring by his brother Dicky and family.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Micky Ward knocks out Shea Neary to win the world title; text overlays state he retired in 2003 and married Charlene in 2005.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Achieved: win the fight and become world champion.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: World champion, with text indicating future marriage to Charlene and retirement in 2003.
evidence_quotes: and goes crashing down to the canvas! Hear Lampley scream, "Big uppercut by Ward! First time in 22 fights that Shea Neary has been down!"

has daughter Kasie
status_updates: becomes world champion
retires from boxing in 2003
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Champion boxer whose victory is shared with his brother and family, leading to announced future stability.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Micky Ward begins as a loyal but frustrated boxer trapped by family obligations, repeatedly sacrificing career opportunities for his unreliable brother Dicky. After a career-threatening injury and depression, he breaks from family control, rebuilds his career with new management, and eventually reconciles with Dicky on his own terms. His journey culminates in winning the world championship while balancing family loyalty with personal independence.
